name: SAM
lore: OpenAI CEO. The Codex side inherits OpenAI's consumer distribution, capital stack, and compute narrative.

dossier.tagline: Distribution and capital gravity for Team Codex.

background.text: OpenAI's capital announcement frames consumer adoption, enterprise deployment, developer usage, and compute as a reinforcing flywheel.

keyProjects.name: OpenAI distribution flywheel
keyProjects.description: The Codex side inherits the reach of ChatGPT, the API, and OpenAI's capital and compute narrative.

strengths.label: Market gravity
strengths.description: Turns company-level momentum into product attention for Codex and adjacent developer workflows.

narrative.title: Market Gravity
narrative.text: A commander class whose special meter charges through distribution, capital, and public narrative pressure.

ATK.rationale: OpenAI distribution, capital, and compute strategy create maximum market offense.

DEF.rationale: OpenAI has security posture, but his visible role is more expansionary than defensive.

SPD.rationale: Public narrative and launch cadence keep OpenAI moving quickly across surfaces.

COMBO.rationale: Combines ChatGPT distribution, API, compute, enterprise, and developer funnels.

HP.rationale: Capital and compute commitments give Codex a huge durability reserve.

threatScore.rationale: Threat comes from market gravity: attention, capital, compute, and distribution moving together.

name: GREG
lore: OpenAI president and co-founder. A technical and launch-adjacent signal source for platform shifts.

background.text: The platform tracks Greg Brockman as a technical and launch-adjacent OpenAI signal source for Codex-side shifts.

keyProjects.name: Codex launch signal
keyProjects.description: Monitored for engineering demos, product claims, and platform direction around OpenAI developer workflows.

strengths.label: Technical demo pressure
strengths.description: Makes capability feel concrete through demos and engineering-forward framing.

narrative.title: Baremetal Combo
narrative.text: A hands-on fighter whose attacks land hardest when the demo is live and the repo is open.

ATK.rationale: Engineering demos and Codex launch signal create direct product-offense pressure.

DEF.rationale: Technical rigor helps, though his role is weighted toward building and shipping.

SPD.rationale: Launch-adjacent engineering communication tends to move fast.

COMBO.rationale: Hands-on demos connect model capability, developer trust, and product proof.

HP.rationale: Co-founder durability and engineering credibility keep him relevant across cycles.

threatScore.rationale: Threat comes from making Codex capability tangible through engineering-forward demos.

name: FIDJI
lore: CEO of Applications at OpenAI. Important for how Codex and ChatGPT turn research into product and business workflows.

dossier.tagline: Applications-side scale operator for OpenAI products.

background.text: OpenAI's public company narrative emphasizes moving capability into consumer, enterprise, and developer products; Fidji Simo is tracked as an applications-side roster signal.

keyProjects.name: Applications scale
keyProjects.description: A product and business-workflow lens for ChatGPT, Codex, and OpenAI's broader applications layer.

strengths.label: Product scaling
strengths.description: Converts model capability into repeatable product surfaces and business workflows.

narrative.title: Applications Scale
narrative.text: A growth-class fighter who turns research unlocks into surfaces people actually use.

ATK.rationale: Applications leadership turns research into product surfaces and business workflows.

DEF.rationale: Scaling applications requires reliability and enterprise controls, but not primarily safety governance.

SPD.rationale: Application rollouts reward fast packaging, launch discipline, and feedback loops.

COMBO.rationale: Links ChatGPT, Codex, enterprise workflows, and product growth channels.

HP.rationale: Applications org depth gives Codex staying power beyond developer-only adoption.

threatScore.rationale: Threat comes from turning frontier capability into products users repeatedly touch.

name: JAKUB
lore: OpenAI chief scientist. Rare posts matter because research direction changes the Codex capability curve.

dossier.tagline: Research direction pressure behind the Codex curve.

background.text: The roster tracks Jakub Pachocki as a high-impact OpenAI research signal whose sparse public activity can still change the Codex capability read.

keyProjects.name: Reasoning-model watchpoint
keyProjects.description: Monitored for research direction that can later show up as stronger agentic coding behavior.

strengths.label: Capability curve
strengths.description: Research-side influence over the models that developer agents rely on.

narrative.title: Reasoning Overdrive
narrative.text: A research-class fighter who buffs Codex from below the visible product layer.

ATK.rationale: Chief-scientist research direction pushes the Codex capability curve from below the product layer.

DEF.rationale: Research discipline supports reliability and evaluation posture.

SPD.rationale: Sparse public signal and long research cycles keep speed lower than builders.

COMBO.rationale: Model research compounds with Codex agent loops and OpenAI infrastructure.

HP.rationale: Frontier research leadership gives durable model-side health.

threatScore.rationale: Threat comes from hidden model upgrades that change what Codex can do before the UI changes.

name: TIBO
lore: Codex builder at OpenAI. High-signal account for practical product direction and developer workflow changes.

background.text: OpenAI's Simplex case study frames Codex as delegated development work, making Codex builders important product-direction signals.

keyProjects.name: Codex ship loop
keyProjects.description: Tracked for signals around planning, patches, review, and end-to-end developer agent workflows.

strengths.label: Implementation signal
strengths.description: Close to practical developer workflow changes where product claims become usable routines.

narrative.title: Patch Chain
narrative.text: A combo fighter whose pressure comes from turning plan into diff into shipped work.

ATK.rationale: Direct Codex building gives him strong developer-workflow offense.

DEF.rationale: Product engineering helps reliability, though security is not the main public signal.

SPD.rationale: Codex workflow shipping and launch loops make speed a core stat.

COMBO.rationale: Combines cloud delegation, CLI, GitHub, and app workflow surfaces.

HP.rationale: OpenAI product infrastructure gives the builder lane durability.

threatScore.rationale: Threat comes from practical Codex shipping pressure in the developer workflow.

name: ROMAIN
lore: Head of Developer Experience at OpenAI, working on Codex and the API. Useful for developer-platform positioning.

dossier.tagline: Developer-experience amplifier for OpenAI's platform surface.

background.text: OpenAI's Codex customer story positions developer workflow as a strategic platform surface; Romain Huet is tracked as a developer-experience roster signal.

keyProjects.name: Developer experience channel
keyProjects.description: Monitored for Codex, API, docs, and developer-platform positioning signals.

strengths.label: Developer translation
strengths.description: Turns platform capability into examples, docs, and adoption hooks.

narrative.title: Just Build Things
narrative.text: A developer-relations caster whose buffs make Codex feel closer to the terminal.

ATK.rationale: Developer-experience leadership makes Codex easier to adopt and explain.

DEF.rationale: Docs, API clarity, and platform hygiene add trust even when not security-specific.

SPD.rationale: DevEx work moves quickly with launch, docs, demos, and community feedback.

COMBO.rationale: Connects APIs, docs, Codex, and developer education into adoption leverage.

HP.rationale: A strong platform surface keeps Codex useful after launch spikes fade.

threatScore.rationale: Threat comes from reducing friction until Codex feels like the default developer path.

name: JASON
lore: Developer Experience Engineer on the Codex team at OpenAI. Creator of Instructor, a Python library for structured LLM outputs.

dossier.tagline: Structured-output specialist bringing schema discipline to Codex.

background.text: Jason Liu says he is a Developer Experience Engineer on the Codex team at OpenAI.
His site says he created Instructor, a Python library for structured LLM outputs that OpenAI cited as inspiration for structured outputs.

keyProjects.name: Instructor
keyProjects.description: Instructor maps LLM outputs to Pydantic models through a response_model workflow and keeps close compatibility with the OpenAI Python SDK.

keyProjects.name: Structured outputs education
keyProjects.description: Weights & Biases lists Jason as the instructor for a course on structured outputs and data validation.

strengths.label: Schema-first product sense
strengths.description: Makes LLM behavior legible to developers by turning freeform text into typed, validated outputs.

narrative.title: Pydantic Lock
narrative.text: A control-class fighter who wins by forcing messy model output into typed lanes before the next combo starts.

ATK.rationale: Structured-output expertise turns messy LLM workflows into practical engineering patterns.

DEF.rationale: Schema validation and Pydantic-style discipline map strongly to reliability.

SPD.rationale: Developer education and practical examples move quickly through the community.

COMBO.rationale: Instructor, structured outputs, and OpenAI DevEx combine into schema-first agent leverage.

HP.rationale: Schema discipline makes workflows more maintainable under real production load.

threatScore.rationale: Threat comes from making Codex workflows more typed, validated, and production-friendly.

name: PETER
lore: Creator of OpenClaw, the viral AI personal assistant. TechCrunch reported he joined OpenAI to work on next-generation personal agents.

dossier.tagline: Viral agent hacker turned OpenAI personal-agents builder.

background.text: TechCrunch reported that Peter Steinberger, creator of OpenClaw, joined OpenAI in February 2026.
TechCrunch describes OpenClaw as an AI personal assistant, previously known as Clawdbot and Moltbot, that achieved viral popularity.

keyProjects.name: OpenClaw
keyProjects.description: A viral open-source personal-agent project that OpenAI said would live in a foundation while Steinberger worked on personal agents.

keyProjects.name: Codex swarm usage
keyProjects.description: PC Gamer reported that a posted dashboard showed 603 billion tokens across 7.6 million requests handled by around 100 Codex instances.

strengths.label: Agent-native experimentation
strengths.description: Builds by stress-testing multi-agent workflows at unusual scale and speed.

narrative.title: OpenClaw Swarm
narrative.text: A chaos-engineer class: weird side project, viral agent loop, then a direct jump into OpenAI's personal-agent lane.

ATK.rationale: OpenClaw and personal-agent work create high chaotic product offense.

DEF.rationale: The profile is exploratory and agentic, with less public trust/compliance signal.

SPD.rationale: Viral side-project velocity and large-scale Codex usage make speed a signature stat.

COMBO.rationale: Personal-agent experiments, OpenClaw, and Codex swarms create unusually high combo pressure.

HP.rationale: OpenAI absorption gives the hacker loop institutional staying power.

threatScore.rationale: Threat comes from pulling viral agent experimentation directly into OpenAI product gravity.
